publicationBoundary: This file records research leads and review decisions. It does not publish lyrics, audio, legal advice or unverified historical claims.

attachmentReview.reason: The uploaded HTML dashboards depend on external CDNs and contain estimated charts and broad rights claims. The uploaded reports are useful research leads but include secondary, commercial and user-generated references that require primary-source verification.

acceptedMetadata: Work-level attribution to Devaraya Swamigal remains the current source-aware attribution.
Project Madurai PM0034_02 is a useful external digital witness for the six Kandar Kavasams.
rejectedOrDeferredClaims: The attachment's 16th-century date is not accepted without an edition-level historical source.
Project Madurai availability is not treated as proof that every modern edition, layout, translation or recording is public domain.
Full lyrics from the attachment are not imported.

externalEvidence.evidenceUse: Authorship heading, digital witness and file-distribution conditions

records.nextAction: Select and document a complete edition, confirm redistribution conditions and complete Tamil proofreading before any expansion.

acceptedMetadata: The Project Madurai digital witness attributes the work to Srimat Sadguru Santhananda Swamigal.
The text identifies Raga Nattai in its heading.
rejectedOrDeferredClaims: The attachment's public-domain classification is not accepted merely because Project Madurai distributes the file.
Project Madurai's file-level distribution conditions must not be converted into an unrestricted excerpt republication claim.
Full lyrics and commercial recordings are not imported.

externalEvidence.evidenceUse: Authorship heading, raga heading, digital witness and distribution conditions

records.nextAction: Confirm author/estate rights and edition permissions before publishing beyond the existing reviewed extract.

acceptedMetadata: The attachment correctly identifies the work as a Thiruppugazh hymn attributed to Arunagirinathar.
The existing repository record remains the canonical record.
rejectedOrDeferredClaims: Attachment transliteration, translation and commentary are not imported because their source and reuse rights are not sufficiently established.
The uploaded HTML's chart and analytical claims are not treated as song metadata.
records.nextAction: Add easy-reading Tamil and documented transliteration only after line-by-line human comparison with the existing canonical Tamil source.

acceptedMetadata: The official Andavan Pichai archive identifies Andavan Pichai as the composer/author.
The official archive describes a 1952 composition context and Raga Attana.
The official archive documents family/estate copyright control.
rejectedOrDeferredClaims: No lyrics, easy-reading Tamil, transliteration or translation are imported.
No commercial recording is imported or linked as playable audio.
The attachment's narrative details require independent editorial review before publication.

externalEvidence.evidenceUse: Official title variants, authorship, composition context, raga and rights boundary

records.nextAction: Request written permission from the rights holder for any lyrics, translation, notation, tune or recording.

acceptedMetadata: The official Andavan Pichai archive identifies lyrics associated with Andavan Pichai and Kamakshi Kuppuswamy and music by Kamakshi Kuppuswamy.
The official archive identifies Raga Lathangi, Adi tala and a 1995 recording context.
The work remains under official archive/estate control.
rejectedOrDeferredClaims: No lyric fragments, transliteration, translation or modern commentary are imported.
No audio or choreography material is imported.
Secondary newspaper and user-generated references are not treated as sufficient republication authority.

externalEvidence.evidenceUse: Official authorship, music, raga, tala, recording and rights boundary

records.nextAction: Obtain written text, translation, notation, choreography and audio permissions separately before publishing any protected material.

globalRules: Project Madurai distribution does not automatically prove that every work is public domain; the project's FAQ states that its collection includes public-domain works and works included with author consent.
An ancient underlying text and a modern edition, translation, notation, arrangement or recording must have separate rights decisions.
No attachment-derived lyrics are published until canonical source, exact edition, proofreading and redistribution rights are recorded.
Commercial audio remains unavailable unless owned, licensed or permission-confirmed.
